title: Oscar Predictions for an Unpredictable Year
text: This prediction piece examines the competitive 2026 Oscar races across major categories ahead of the 98th Academy Awards ceremony on March 15. The article identifies One Battle After Another (directed by Paul Thomas Anderson) as the frontrunner despite recent uncertainty. The film boasts an impressive awards résumé, having won the Producers Guild Award, Golden Globes, Directors Guild, BAFTAs, and Writers Guild awards. Its primary challenger, Sinners, secured the Actor Award for Best Ensemble, creating speculation about a potential upset. Despite this late momentum shift, One Battle After Another's awards résumé simply dwarfs Sinners'. Historical precedent suggests films with comparable trophy collections haven't lost Best Picture. Best Director: Paul Thomas Anderson predicted to win. Best Actress: Jessie Buckley (Hamnet) identified as one of the ceremony's clearest locks. Best Actor: Michael B. Jordan (Sinners) noted as current favorite, though the race remains volatile. Best Supporting Actress/Actor: Multiple contenders maintain viable paths to victory. Screenplay Categories: Predictions suggest Sinners wins Original and One Battle After Another captures Adapted. The article emphasizes unpredictability across acting categories, describing them as practically toss-ups with shifting momentum throughout the awards season.
